mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate above a 0.2 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 52% over 5-year walk-forward tests).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions (t-stat < 1.5 over 10-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real interest rates and signals monetary policy lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in > 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 70% of confirmed recession quarters despite the GDP contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment signals a supply-demand mismatch and economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns (out-of-sample directional accuracy < 50% over 5-year walk-forward tests).
